rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Ni-catalyzed 1,4-diarylation of 1,3-dienes with aryl iodides to form 1,4-diaryl-2-alkenes
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Ni(II) precatalyst to active low-valent Ni(I)/Ni(0); also reduces Ni(III)-aryl intermediate B to Ni(I) C during the cycle

key_intermediates.name: Ni(I)-Ln (active catalyst A in favored cycle)
Ni(I)-allyl/alkyl species D from migratory insertion of 1,3-diene
Ar-Ni(III)(X)-allyl intermediate E (after second oxidative addition)
Ni(0)-Ln (alternative active species in Ni(0)/Ni(II) cycle)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II)X2 to Ni(I) (A); A undergoes oxidative addition with ArI to give Ni(III)-Ar (B), which is cathodically reduced to ArNi(I) (C); 1,3-diene migratory insertion gives allyl-Ni(I) (D), a second ArI oxidative addition yields Ni(III) intermediate E, and reductive elimination delivers the 1,4-diaryl product 3 with regeneration of Ni(I) A.

probe_reagents.name: NiBr2·diglyme (CV of catalyst)
probe_reagents.observation: Redox peak at E1/2 = -1.39 V (vs SCE) assigned to Ni(II)/Ni(I)

probe_reagents.name: NiBr2·diglyme + 1a (diene)
probe_reagents.observation: Two peaks at -1.39 V (Ni(II)/Ni(I)) and -2.60 V (free diene); no significant interaction of Ni(I)X with diene

probe_reagents.name: NiBr2·diglyme + 1a + 2aa (PhI)
probe_reagents.observation: Disappearance of -1.39 V peak and appearance of -2.60 V (Ni(I)/Ni(0)) peak, indicating Ni(I)X is the species that undergoes oxidative addition with ArI to form ArNi(III)X

probe_reagents.name: Iodobenzene (2aa) CV
probe_reagents.observation: E1/2 = -2.50 V (vs SCE); more easily reduced than diene

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric reaction without current
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeds, indicating Ni(0)-initiated pathway is also feasible

notes: Authors propose two cycles: (favored) Ni(I)/Ni(III) pathway supported by CV showing Ni(I)X is the species engaging ArI; (alternative) Ni(0)/Ni(II) pathway not ruled out, supported by stoichiometric reaction proceeding without current — Ni(0) A inserts into ArI to form Ni(II)-Ar (B), then cathodic reduction to Ni(I) C, migratory insertion with diene to D, oxidative addition of second ArI to E (Ni(III)), reductive elimination yields product 3 and Ni(I) F, which is cathodically reduced back to Ni(0) A.
